sentence: Subjective:
The patient, Mrs. Kelly Wood, presented with hematuria, which was confirmed by a urine dipstick test.
She reported passing two small stones last week, and for the past six weeks, she has experienced episodes of bright red urine, which she described as the color of cherry Kool-Aid.
She also reported having to urinate frequently and experiencing severe pain, rating it an 8 out of 10.
She denied having any flank pain or tenderness in her lower back after passing the stones.
She is taking Alavert 10mg daily and has no drug allergies.
She admitted to not drinking enough water, consuming more Mountain Dew than water, and not following a balanced diet.
Objective:
Vital signs: Not mentioned.
Physical exam: The patient had a normal abdominal examination with no flank pain.
The anti-GU was normal, and the vaginal exam was normal.
Test Results:
The urine dipstick test was negative for leukocytes, nitrates, protein, ketone, bilirubin, and glucose.
The urine was yellow and clear, with a pH of 5.5, specific gravity of 1.020, and urobilinogen of 0.2.

The renal ultrasound showed a 6mm left renal stone that is non-obstructing, with the right kidney measuring 10cm in length by 5cm in width, and the left kidney measuring 10.8cm in length by 5cm in width.
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient has hematuria due to a 6mm left renal stone that is not obstructing and is currently asymptomatic.
Problem list: Hematuria; Urolithiasis; Chronic urolithiasis.
Plan:
I advised the patient to drink 125 ounces of water per day, avoid sugary drinks like Mountain Dew, and reduce her sodium intake to less than 1,000mg per day.
I also recommended avoiding food rich in sodium oxalate and animal protein, such as salty, processed foods, beef, poultry, fish, and pork.
I provided her with a handout outlining the dietary recommendations and what to avoid.
I instructed her to return next year unless she experiences any further issues like bleeding or pain, in which case she can come back sooner.
Follow-up:
I scheduled a follow-up appointment for next year, or sooner if she experiences any further issues.
